verse_data.topic: Classification of Navamsa based on sign type and position

content_blocks.text:
चरभवनेष्वाद्यंशा स्थिरेषु मध्या द्विमूर्तिषु तथाम्स्याः ।
वर्गोत्तमाः प्रदिष्टास्तेष्विह जातः कुले मुख्याः ॥

verse_data.sanskrit_text:
चरभवनेष्वाद्यंशा स्थिरेषु मध्या द्विमूर्तिषु तथाम्स्याः ।
वर्गोत्तमाः प्रदिष्टास्तेष्विह जातः कुले मुख्याः ॥
verse_data.topic: Classification of Navamsa based on sign type and position
Classification of Navamsa based on sign type and position
Rules for Dasamamsa and Dwadasamsa lords

content_blocks.text:
Sloka 35. In the case of an odd sign, the owners
of the Dasamamsa (दशमोश) or roth portions are to be
counted from the sign itself; in the case of an even
sign, from its 9th onwards. The owners of the
Dwadasamsa (द्वादशोश) or th portions of a sign are
to be reckoned from that sign.

verse_data.translation:
Sloka 35. In the case of an odd sign, the owners
of the Dasamamsa (दशमोश) or roth portions are to be
counted from the sign itself; in the case of an even
sign, from its 9th onwards. The owners of the
Dwadasamsa (द्वादशोश) or th portions of a sign are
to be reckoned from that sign.

content_blocks.text:
NOTES.
This sloka gives the rules for calculating the Dasamamsa
(दशमांश) and Dwadasamsa (द्वादशांश) of a Rasi.

दिगंशया ततश्चौजे युग्मे तश्चवमाद्वदेत् ।
पूर्वादि वश दिक्पाला इन्द्राग्नियमराक्षसाः ॥
वरुणो मारुतश्चैव कुबेरेशानपद्मजाः ।
अनन्तश्च क्रमादोजे समे वा व्युत्क्रमेण तु ॥
द्वादशांशस्य गणना तत्तत्क्षेत्राद्विनिर्दिशेत् ।
तेषामधीशाः क्रमशो गणेशाऽश्वियमा हयः ॥

verse_data.sanskrit_text:
दिगंशया ततश्चौजे युग्मे तश्चवमाद्वदेत् ।
पूर्वादि वश दिक्पाला इन्द्राग्नियमराक्षसाः ॥
वरुणो मारुतश्चैव कुबेरेशानपद्मजाः ।
अनन्तश्च क्रमादोजे समे वा व्युत्क्रमेण तु ॥
द्वादशांशस्य गणना तत्तत्क्षेत्राद्विनिर्दिशेत् ।
तेषामधीशाः क्रमशो गणेशाऽश्वियमा हयः ॥
verse_data.topic: Rules for Dasamamsa and Dwadasamsa lords

content_blocks.text:
It will be seen from the above that according to Parasara
(पराशर) (a) the lords of the Dasamamsas in the case of an odd sign
are (Indra (इन्द्र), (2) Agni (अग्नि), (3) Yama (यग), (4) Rakshasa,
(5) Varuna (वरुण), (6) Maruta (मान्त), (7) Kubera (कुबेर), (8) Esana
(ईशान), (9) Padmaja (पद्मज), and (10) Anantha (अनन्त), these being
counted in thetinverse order in the case of an even sign; and

(5) Varuna (वरुण), (6) Maruta (मान्त), (7) Kubera (कुबेर), (8) Esana

counted in thetinverse order in the case of an even sign; and
verse_data.topic: Explanation of Dasamamsa lords according to Parasara

raw_text:
22
आतकपारिजाते
Adh. I
वर्गोत्तमाश्चरगृहादिषु पूर्वमध्यपर्यन्ततः शुभफला नवभागसज्ञाः ॥
"Also सत्य
चरभवनेष्वाद्यंशा स्थिरेषु मध्या द्विमूर्तिषु तथाम्स्याः ।
वर्गोत्तमाः प्रदिष्टास्तेष्विह जातः कुले मुख्याः ॥
Also सारावली
वर्गोत्तमा नवांशास्तथादिमध्यान्तगाश्चरायेषु ।
स्तौ कुलमुख्यकराः ॥
लग्नादिदशमांशशास्त्वोजे युग्मे शुभादिकाः ।
द्वादशांशाधिपतयस्तत्तद्राारीवशानुगाः ॥ ३५ ॥
Sloka 35. In the case of an odd sign, the owners
of the Dasamamsa (दशमोश) or roth portions are to be
counted from the sign itself; in the case of an even
sign, from its 9th onwards. The owners of the
Dwadasamsa (द्वादशोश) or th portions of a sign are
to be reckoned from that sign.
NOTES.
This sloka gives the rules for calculating the Dasamamsa
(दशमांश) and Dwadasamsa (द्वादशांश) of a Rasi.
cf. पराशर
